resources.string: A new eye opens on the spine, blessing the flesh that failed.

Soft tendrils press through the seams of armor like eager brushstrokes. Feathers grow brilliant and sharp enough to cut. Bones loosen, shift into new angles, and sing as they fuse anew. The scream is pain and gratitude braided together. Do not call it deformity. A fixed body is a prison for unborn miracles, and this flesh has finally learned to become art.
